corpusSplit.note: requirement 9: distinguish corpus record count (1,000,000) from actual trainer split counts. Split is a deterministic fnv1a(canonicalKey)%100 partition of corpus_expanded1m.jsonl: train0-89, validation90-94, frozen95-99. Identical across the bridge and full runs (same corpus, same hash).

memoryStreamingDecision.reasoning: load_corpus() still uses fs::read_to_string on the full 1.6GB JSONL followed by building Vec<DistillRecord>. Smoke tests loading the full corpus_expanded1m.jsonl completed in 21-43s across --train-subset-size 2000/500000 with max-epochs 1-60 on a 16GB-RAM/~8.5GB-free-at-start machine, with no OOM, no swapping, and no abnormal slowdown. train/src/t090_distillation.rs was left unchanged (no code diff in this task).

resumeVerification.method: scratch run train/data/t127d/resume-check (200k train subset, not part of the official curve), interrupted mid-epoch-7 by an out-of-band process termination after epoch-06.bin/.state were saved, then re-invoked with the identical command
resumeVerification.result: printed 'resume mix=teacher-only seed=1 epoch=6' and continued training to completion (exit code 0)

oneMillionFull.seedNote: All three seeds chose the identical engine move on all 60 oracle positions (verified by direct per-position move comparison), despite having distinct weight-file SHA-256 hashes and small differences in frozen_mean_regret (5.203/5.189/5.146). The zero seed-SD on this 60-position oracle reflects policy agreement on this specific test set, not weight-identical models.

fittedPowerLaw.model: regret = a * N^(-k), nonlinear least squares

logLinear.model: regret = a + b*log10(N), ordinary least squares (requirement-9 primary refit)

refit5Point.note: Fit quality (R2) dropped from the T126 3-point fits (0.994-1.000) to 0.929-0.987 here because the 500k/900k points sit above (worse than) what the 3-point curve predicted (predicted ~1.85-1.98 at 500k and ~1.47-1.68 at 900k vs. measured 2.4 and 1.9). This is a sensitivity analysis, not a statistical confidence interval.

designReportDecisionRuleSection7.ruleZone: measured mean (1.9) is above the discontinue threshold (1.70) from tasks/design/T127-corpus-1m-report.md section 7; this is reported as objective context for T127e, not a T127d acceptance/production decision (out of scope for this task)

cargoTest.result: PASS (56 lib unit + 2 teacher_candidates unit + 1 real_data, 0 failed)
cargoTest.note: no train/src changes were made in this task (see memoryStreamingDecision); this run is a baseline/regression confirmation only

scopeNote: This task covers train/ side experiments and this report only. Acceptance/adoption decisions, game-play gating, and app/engine wiring are out of scope (T127e). bench/edax-compare/*.py generator files were not modified (T143 concurrent scope).

addendum.requestedBy: orchestrator, post-done, per verifier/codex-review recommendation, as input to T127e

kDensityProbe180k.purpose: discriminate whether the 1M curve's underperformance vs. T126's 3-point extrapolation is caused by K=4 same-game density expansion (corpus_expanded1m) vs. pure diminishing returns, by comparing at equal N=180k

pairedDirectComparison.method: position-level paired bootstrap on (K4 regret - K1 regret) using the same 60 t096 position ids, seed 96002, 100000 samples
pairedDirectComparison.caveat: the two runs used different eval_cli builds (T126: 6ba26dc5f895f23a5b51e7299c47163e10f678bb282f0d12326f11ee4236b2b8, T127d: e874bb4c434125a4a996ffebb24c8ffcce6f535b706d2eef3baa21f70ccd740a); both reproduced v2=1.5666666666666667 exactly, so scoring behavior is consistent across builds even though they are not byte-identical

kDensityProbe180k.preregisteredInterpretationRule: same-N regret near 2.77 => pure diminishing returns; clearly worse (example threshold >3.0) => K-density explanation
kDensityProbe180k.conclusion: measured 4.0000 is above the 3.0 pre-registered threshold and 1.2333 discs worse than the K=1 180k point, so the point estimate falls in the K-density zone; however the direct paired-difference 95% CI straddles zero, so statistical significance at only 60 oracle positions is not established

absoluteRegretBootstrapCi.purpose: quantify how statistically sharp the 1.9000 > 1.70 discontinue-threshold exceedance is, using only the already-collected oracle JSON (no new training/Edax calls)
absoluteRegretBootstrapCi.method: position-level percentile bootstrap of the absolute candidate mean regret, seed 96002, 100000 samples (same convention as compare_pattern_v3.py, applied to the raw regret values instead of the v2-candidate difference)

oneMillion.note: seed 1 used as representative; seeds 2 and 3 have position-identical regret arrays (see main report), so the CI is the same for all three

absoluteRegretBootstrapCi.conclusion: both the 1M and 500k absolute-regret 95% CIs straddle the 1.70 discontinue threshold (and even straddle v2's own CI), so the exceedance is a point-estimate fact rather than a statistically sharp one at n=60 oracle positions
